items.headline: Tatum Out with Torn Achilles; Embiid, SGA Also Sidelined
items.summary: The Boston Celtics face a major setback as Jayson Tatum is reportedly out with a torn right Achilles tendon, with an expected return around March 10. The Philadelphia 76ers' Joel Embiid also remains sidelined. Meanwhile, Oklahoma City's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander is questionable for tonight's game due to an abdominal strain.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Questionable for Blazers with Back Injury
items.summary: Portland Trail Blazers forward Deni Avdija, an Israeli standout averaging 24.4 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 6.6 assists this season, was listed as questionable for yesterday's game against the Memphis Grizzlies due to a back injury. His status for upcoming games will be crucial as the Blazers push for a play-in spot.

items.headline: Hawks Extend Win Streak, Top Bucks
items.summary: The Atlanta Hawks secured their fifth consecutive win, moving above .500 for the first time since December, by defeating the Milwaukee Bucks 131-113.

items.headline: Thunder Edge Knicks in Close Contest
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder edged out the New York Knicks with a 103-100 victory.

items.headline: Clippers Rout Slumping Pacers
items.summary: The Los Angeles Clippers notched their third straight win, routing the Indiana Pacers 130-107, who have now lost seven in a row.

items.headline: Lakers Hold Off Pelicans at Home
items.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers defeated the New Orleans Pelicans 110-101 in a hard-fought contest.

items.headline: Jrue Holiday's Double-Double Fuels Blazers
items.statContext: Led Portland in their win over Memphis.

items.headline: Chet Holmgren Dominates for OKC
items.statContext: Pivotal in the Thunder's win over New York.

items.headline: Kawhi Leonard Leads Clippers Attack
items.statContext: Helped the Clippers to their third consecutive win.

items.headline: Pascal Siakam Returns with Strong Performance
items.statContext: Led the Pacers in scoring upon returning from injury.

items.headline: Alexander-Walker Shines for Hawks
items.statContext: Top scorer in Atlanta's win against Milwaukee.

items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo's Return
items.statContext: In his second game back from injury.

items.headline: Luka Doncic Leads NBA Scoring
items.statContext: Currently leads the league in points per game.

items.headline: Lakers Face Nuggets in Critical Western Conference Showdown
items.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers (37-24), currently 6th in the West, head to Denver to battle the Nuggets (38-24) tonight. The Lakers are only a half-game behind Denver in the standings, making this a pivotal matchup. Denver is dealing with several injuries, including Aaron Gordon (hamstring) and Peyton Watson (hamstring) being out, and Cam Johnson (ankle) questionable.

items.headline: Trade Deadline Aftermath: Key Moves Reshaping Rosters
items.summary: While the 2026 NBA Trade Deadline passed on February 5th, the impact of several major deals continues to be felt across the league. Notable moves included James Harden to the Cavaliers, Ivica Zubac to the Pacers, Trae Young to the Wizards, Jaren Jackson Jr. to the Jazz, and Anthony Davis to the Wizards. Kawhi Leonard remained with the Clippers despite trade rumors.

items.headline: Bucks Continue to Slide in Eastern Conference
items.summary: The Milwaukee Bucks have lost their last four games by a combined 97 points, falling five games behind Atlanta and Charlotte, who are tied for ninth in the Eastern Conference standings. Giannis Antetokounmpo, despite scoring 24 points last night, could not stop the skid.

items.headline: Tonight's Key Matchups Across the League
items.summary: Tonight's NBA schedule features several intriguing contests, including the Heat vs. Nets, Magic vs. Mavericks, Rockets vs. Warriors, and the Raptors vs. Timberwolves.
